decision_summary: The improved AOF overview artifact is materially better because it names the buyer problem, grounds claims in evidence, and clarifies the next action.
rationale: The before version stayed generic and abstract, while the after version explains the operator problem in plain language, points to benchmark/runtime evidence, and makes the trust case more concrete.
recommendation: Accept the improved artifact and preserve the same buyer-facing review discipline for future external-facing materials.
target_audience: A first-time operator or buyer deciding whether AOF is concrete enough to trust.
expected_user_reaction: The improved version should feel more credible because it explains the problem, the proof surface, and the concrete next step.
artifact_change_recommendations: Keep the buyer problem statement near the top of the artifact.
Prefer concrete benchmark/runtime proof over abstract claims of comprehensiveness.
organization_change_recommendations: Retain buyer-facing review criteria as a required gate for external-facing artifacts.
Require before/after evidence when claiming that a review loop materially improved quality.
human_override_signal: Owner expectation: the review must explain why the later artifact earns more trust from a first-time reader.
